title: Guardrail
description:
Top-level `Guardrail` resource shape. Mirrors what cp-api writes to kine at `/aisix/<env>/guardrails/<uuid>`.

`deny_unknown_fields` is intentionally NOT set here: serde's `flatten` + `tag = "kind"` interaction can't pass the "I consumed this field" signal up to the outer struct, so a `deny_unknown_fields` outer would reject the very `kind` the inner enum needs. Strict typo-rejection happens earlier in the JSON Schema (`schema::validate_guardrail`) which the loader runs before deserialise on every watch event.

patterns.description: Blocklist patterns. Empty list is legal but pointless â€” the guardrail will allow every request, same as `enabled: false`.

oneOf.description: AWS Bedrock managed guardrail. The chain builder constructs a `BedrockGuardrail` that calls AWS `ApplyGuardrail` (real SigV4 dispatch) on input and/or output per the row's hook point.

aws_credentials.description: IAM credentials. v1 = static access keys (encrypted).

region.description: AWS region the Bedrock endpoint lives in (e.g. `us-east-1`).

oneOf.description: Azure AI Content Safety Prompt Shield. Detects jailbreak and indirect injection attacks via the `/contentsafety/text:shieldPrompt` API. P1 (PRD-09c Â§6 P1).

api_key.description: Subscription key (`Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key`). Decrypted by cp-api before kine projection; plaintext in memory only, never logged.

endpoint.description: Azure Cognitive Services resource endpoint, e.g. `https://my-resource.cognitiveservices.azure.com`. The DP appends `/contentsafety/text:shieldPrompt?api-version=2024-09-01`.

timeout_ms.description:
HTTP call timeout in milliseconds. When elapsed the `fail_open` flag governs the verdict. Defaults to 5 000 ms.

**`0` does not mean "no timeout".** `Duration::ZERO` causes `tokio::time::timeout` to fire on the first poll, so every call immediately maps to a timeout failure. Use `u32::MAX` (â‰ˆ 49 days) for an effectively unlimited timeout.

oneOf.description: Azure AI Content Safety Text Moderation. Category-severity + blocklist moderation via the `/contentsafety/text:analyze` API, on input and/or output (including streaming output). P2 (PRD-09c Â§6 P2, #379).

api_key.description: Subscription key (`Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key`). Plaintext in memory only, never logged.

blocklist_names.description: Azure CS blocklist names to match against.

categories.description: Categories to analyze. Defaults to all four.

endpoint.description: Azure Cognitive Services resource endpoint. The DP appends `/contentsafety/text:analyze?api-version=2024-09-01`.

max_buffer_bytes.description: Max bytes buffered in `buffer_full` mode before `on_buffer_exceeded` applies. Default 262 144.

on_buffer_exceeded.description: `fail_closed` (default) or `fail_open` when the buffer cap is hit.

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the OUTPUT hook. Defaults `false` (fail-closed) so an Azure outage can't release unscanned model output.

severity_threshold.description: General severity threshold; a category at or above it blocks.

severity_threshold_by_category.description: Per-category threshold overrides (take precedence over the general one).

text_source.description: Input-hook text selection: `concatenate_user_content` (default) or `concatenate_all_content`. Ignored on the output hook.

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout (ms); `fail_open` / `output_fail_open` govern the verdict when it elapses. See the `AzureContentSafetyConfig` note on why `0` means "fire immediately", not "no timeout".

window_overlap_size.description: Chars carried between windows so a span split across a boundary is still caught. Default 256.

window_size.description: Sliding-window size in chars (window mode); cp-api caps it at the 10 000-char Azure limit. Default 10 000.

oneOf.description: Aliyun content-safety guardrail. Risk-level moderation via the `TextModerationPlus` action on `green-cip.<region>.aliyuncs.com`, on input and/or output (including streaming output). #603.

access_key_secret.description: Aliyun AccessKey secret. Decrypted by cp-api before kine projection; plaintext in memory only, never logged. Used to sign the request.

endpoint.description: Explicit endpoint override (full URL, no trailing slash). When set it wins over `region`; used by tests/dev to point at a mock server.

max_buffer_bytes.description: Max bytes buffered in `buffer_full` mode before `on_buffer_exceeded` applies. Default 262 144.

on_buffer_exceeded.description: `fail_closed` (default) or `fail_open` when the buffer cap is hit.

output_fail_open.description: Fail-open policy for the OUTPUT hook. Defaults `false` (fail-closed) so an Aliyun outage can't release unscanned model output.

region.description: Aliyun region the guardrail lives in, e.g. `cn-shanghai`. The DP builds the endpoint `https://green-cip.<region>.aliyuncs.com`.

risk_level_threshold.description: Minimum risk level that triggers a block: `low`, `medium`, or `high` (default). A returned level at or above this blocks.

timeout_ms.description: HTTP call timeout (ms); `fail_open` / `output_fail_open` govern the verdict when it elapses. See the `AzureContentSafetyConfig` note on why `0` means "fire immediately", not "no timeout".

window_overlap_size.description: Chars carried between windows so a span split across a boundary is still caught. Default 128.

window_size.description: Sliding-window size in chars (window mode). Default 2 000 â€” Aliyun's `llm_response_moderation` per-call content cap.

direction.description:
Which traffic directions this guardrail applies to when resolved through an attachment. Values: `"input"`, `"output"`, `"both"` (default).

Stored and forwarded to the CP dashboard. Direction-based filtering in `GuardrailIndex::resolve` is not yet implemented; the `hook_point` field on the guardrail definition provides equivalent per-hook-point control for keyword rules.

enabled.description: When false the chain skips this rule entirely. Lets operators stage a rule (write it, sanity-check it via dry runs, then flip it on) without deleting + recreating.

enforcement_mode.description: How the DP behaves when this guardrail fires. `"block"` (default) â€” reject the request. `"monitor"` â€” let the request through and record the event (**not yet implemented**; the DP currently always blocks regardless of this field â€” do not set `"monitor"` expecting pass-through behavior until a future release wires it into the chain).

fail_open.description: Behavior when a remote-API guardrail (today `kind=bedrock`) can't reach its upstream. `true` lets the request through (recorded in usage_events.guardrail_bypassed_reason); `false` blocks with 422. No-op for `kind=keyword`. Defaults `true` (matches the PG schema default + PRD-09c Â§6.4).

hook_point.description: Where in the lifecycle this rule runs. Defaults to `both`.

mandatory.description:
When `true`, a runtime error in this guardrail's evaluation (e.g. a Bedrock timeout) is treated as fatal: the request is blocked regardless of `fail_open`. When `false` (default), `fail_open` governs the error path.

**Not yet implemented** â€” the field is stored and forwarded to the CP dashboard but the DP does not yet consult it; `fail_open` alone governs error behavior in the current release.

name.description: Operator-facing name; surfaces in metric labels + error reasons.

BedrockAWSCredentials.description:
AWS credentials for `kind: "bedrock"`. Phase 2 supports `static` (access-key pair); Phase 4 adds `role_arn` (sts:AssumeRole) under the same tag.

Wire shape on the kine path is plaintext: cp-api decrypts the envelope-encrypted secret at projection time (same trust boundary as `provider_keys` â€” see PRD-09c Â§6.3). The DP only ever holds plaintext in memory; it does not need a master key.

secret_access_key.description: Decrypted by cp-api before kine projection; plaintext in memory only, never logged. The DP feeds it to the AWS SDK's static credentials provider.

BedrockLatencyMode.description: Per-guardrail latency policy for `kind: "bedrock"`. `serial` waits unconditionally; `timed` aborts at `timeout_ms` and applies the row-level `fail_open` flag. Range matches cp-api's validator (100..5000ms) â€” see PRD-09c Â§6.6.

GuardrailHookPoint.description: What part of the request lifecycle a guardrail inspects.

oneOf.description: Run on the request payload before bridge dispatch.
Run on the upstream response before the cache write + render.
Run on both. Default for keyword blocklists.

KeywordPattern.description: One pattern in a `keyword`-kind guardrail's blocklist. The DP translates `Literal` to a case-insensitive substring match and `Regex` to a compiled `regex::Regex`. Invalid regex at parse time is loader-rejected (the DP refuses to apply a guardrail it can't compile, so a typo doesn't silently disarm the policy).
